paper_title: Anti-tumor efficacy of a potent and selective non-covalent KRASG12D inhibitor
hypothesis: MRTX1133 is a high-affinity, non-covalent small molecule that selectively inhibits KRASG12D over wild-type KRAS and demonstrates therapeutic efficacy in KRASG12D-mutant cancers

stages.description: Generate 3D structures of KRASG12D mutant and wild-type KRAS proteins for binding analysis

jobs.description: Predict structure of KRASG12D mutant protein
validates_claims: KRASG12D protein structure enables selective inhibitor binding

jobs.description: Predict structure of wild-type KRAS protein for selectivity comparison
validates_claims: Structural differences between KRASG12D and KRASWT enable selectivity

stages.description: Evaluate binding affinity and selectivity of MRTX1133 to KRASG12D vs wild-type KRAS

jobs.description: Dock MRTX1133 to KRASG12D to validate high-affinity binding
validates_claims: MRTX1133 binds with high affinity to KRASG12D (KD ~0.2 pM)
jobs.submission_error: 400 Client Error: Bad Request for url: https://app.tamarind.bio/api/submit-job

jobs.description: Dock MRTX1133 to wild-type KRAS to validate selectivity
validates_claims: MRTX1133 shows ~700-fold selectivity for KRASG12D over KRASWT
jobs.submission_error: 400 Client Error: Bad Request for url: https://app.tamarind.bio/api/submit-job

stages.description: Simulate protein-ligand complex stability to validate binding mechanism

jobs.description: MD simulation of MRTX1133-KRASG12D complex to validate stable binding
validates_claims: MRTX1133 binding induces conformational changes in switch I and II regions
MRTX1133 maintains stable binding to both active and inactive KRASG12D states

MRTX1133 shows ~700-fold selectivity for KRASG12D over KRASWT
MRTX1133 binding induces conformational changes in switch I and II regions
